title: Data Dictionary for nyc_311.csv
description:
**Description**

This dataset contains 10 000 unique New York City 311 complaint records collected between January 2010 and December 2020. Each record is identified by a system‑generated integer key with no duplicates. The complaints span a wide range of incident types—noise, heat/hot water, illegal parking and other miscellaneous issues—with the “Other” category comprising more than half of all complaints.  The majority of incidents are reported in boroughs such as Brooklyn, Queens and Manhattan, with ZIP codes and street names predominating the location fields.  Complaint creation dates cluster around the mid‑2010s, while many closure dates are missing (about 3 % of records).  Resolution narratives and additional details appear in free‑text fields, often containing long descriptions or agency‐specific language.  Geographic coordinates (latitude/longitude and State Plane X/Y) are available for most records, enabling spatial analysis across the five boroughs.  Data entry is highly variable: many “Other” values appear in fields with large cardinality, and several address components are missing.

**Notable Characteristics**

- **Temporal spread:** Created dates range from 01 Jan 2010 to 22 Dec 2020, with a mean around late 2015; closure dates have a median of ~13 days after creation, but 2.7 % are not yet closed.
- **High‑cardinality “Other” categories:** Fields such as `Descriptor`, `Incident Address`, and most geographic fields have an “Other (N)” bucket due to a large number of unique entries, masking fine‑grained analysis.
- **Missing values:** 1.8 % of `Incident Address`, 6.0 % of `City`, and 65.4 % of `Due Date` are null, which may bias analyses that depend on these attributes.
- **Geographic concentration:** Latitude and longitude span the five NYC boroughs; State Plane coordinates show a dense cluster near Manhattan but also include outliers outside typical city boundaries (e.g., coordinates in the 90 000 range).
- **Data quality:** The presence of free‑text in `Resolution Description` introduces noise and potential inconsistencies; the same for `Descriptor` and `Agency Name`.  The dataset contains no duplicates (unique key) but does contain PII/PHI such as full addresses and contact details that should be handled with care.
- **Distribution shape:** Complaint types follow a highly skewed distribution, with noise complaints dominating and “Other” complaints forming the tail.  Date fields show a slight right‑skew (more recent complaints).
- **Temporal ordering:** The `Created Date` and `Closed Date` fields are chronologically ordered per record, allowing time‑to‑resolution calculations.
- **Linkability:** Borough, City, and Community Board fields provide hierarchical geographic grouping; latitude/longitude coordinates can be linked to GIS layers.

---

Unique Key.title: Record Number
Unique Key.description: A system-generated integer that uniquely identifies each complaint record.

Created Date.title: Complaint Creation Date
Created Date.description: The timestamp when the complaint was first recorded in the system.

Closed Date.title: Complaint Closure Date
Closed Date.description: The timestamp when the complaint was closed or marked resolved.

Agency.title: Agency Code
Agency.description: The short code for the agency responsible for the complaint (e.g., NYPD, DOT).

Agency Name.title: Agency Full Name
Agency Name.description: The full name of the agency handling the complaint.
